1	"""P4 — pre-skip identity failures before LLM mining.
2	
3	`evidence_miner._mining_units()` now asks the ONE universal resolver
4	(`Graph.resolve_attribution(mid, policy)`) at the top of every manifestation iteration and, switching
5	ONLY on the structured `reason_code`, drops the manifestations whose identity can never be attributed
6	into four separately-counted buckets BEFORE any LLM is paid to mine them:
7	
8	    IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED       -> identity_unresolved_lead
9	    IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK   -> different_work_quarantine
10	    IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT  -> identity_integrity_quarantine
11	    DERIVATION_CONFLICT       -> derivation_conflict_quarantine
12	
13	This is ONLY a spend optimization — card construction still calls the same resolver, so nothing that
14	this selector lets through can be attributed without passing the identical gate. In particular a
15	`VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED` preprint is NOT pre-skipped: a verified exact-span correspondence may still
16	name a permitted expression for one span even when the whole manifestation cannot.
17	
18	Every fixture runs through the REAL chain — `migrate()`/`ingest_bytes` -> `derive_binding_core` ->
19	`resolve_attribution`. No Attribution is mocked and no verdict is hand-assigned.
20	
21	Generality (Sol global rules 1 & 6): the selector keys on the machine `reason_code` alone, never on a
22	DOI, title, author, venue, or subject literal. The metamorphic parameters replace every identifier and
23	subject term while holding the STRUCTURE constant; unit selection and the four counters are identical
24	across all of them.
25	"""
26	import json
27	
28	import pytest
29	
30	import provenance as P
31	import evidence_miner as EM
32	from provenance import (RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED, RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK,
33	                        RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT, RC_DERIVATION_CONFLICT, RC_VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED,
34	                        RC_ADMITTED, JOURNAL_ONLY, ANY_VERSION)
35	
36	
37	FILLER = ('This paper studies the question at length across many pages of careful analysis, '
38	          'reporting the design, the data and the estimates in full. ') * 120
39	# A long, self-contained body that self-identifies NOWHERE — no DOI, no title echo, no byline cue. It
40	# is long enough to be a complete document, so its ONLY reason to be skipped is unproven identity.
41	NEUTRAL = ('The section opens with the general setting and the notation used throughout, describing '
42	           'the framework in broad terms before any numeric quantity is reported below. ') * 130
43	
44	JOURNAL_MASTHEAD = 'Some Learned Review, Volume 12, Number 3, 2020, Pages 45-67.'
45	PREPRINT_STAMP   = 'arXiv:2401.01234'
46	
47	# Four unrelated Work identities. NO rule under test may branch on any of these strings.
48	DOMAINS = [
49	    dict(id='clinical', title='A Randomised Trial of the Widget Regimen in Adults',
50	         authors=('Adams', 'Brown'), byline='By Alice Adams and Bob Brown', doi='10.1056/clin-A',
51	         venue='New England Journal of Medicine', foreign_doi='10.9999/clin-STRANGER'),
52	    dict(id='legal', title='On the Interpretation of the Contract Formation Doctrine',
53	         authors=('Reyes', 'Silva'), byline='By Ana Reyes and Ivo Silva', doi='10.2307/legal-A',
54	         venue='Harvard Law Review', foreign_doi='10.9999/legal-STRANGER'),
55	    dict(id='economics', title='Automation and the Structure of New Labour Tasks',
56	         authors=('Okoro', 'Tan'), byline='By Ngozi Okoro and Wei Tan', doi='10.1086/econ-A',
57	         venue='Journal of Political Economy', foreign_doi='10.9999/econ-STRANGER'),
58	    dict(id='cs', title='A Transformer Architecture for Program Synthesis Tasks',
59	         authors=('Park', 'Vasquez'), byline='By Jin Park and Luis Vasquez', doi='10.5555/cs-A',
60	         venue='NeurIPS Proceedings', foreign_doi='10.9999/cs-STRANGER'),
61	]
62	
63	
64	def _scholarly_body(d, *, furniture=''):
65	    """A complete scholarly body that self-identifies (matching front-matter DOI + byline) and carries
66	    the given structural version `furniture`."""
67	    return (f'{d["title"]}\n{d["byline"]}\n{furniture}\n'
68	            f'{JOURNAL_MASTHEAD} doi: {d["doi"]}\n'
69	            f'1. Introduction\n{FILLER}\n4. Results\nWe estimate the effect is 0.2 units.\n')
70	
71	
72	def _row(d, *, fulltext):
73	    return {'doi': d['doi'], 'title': d['title'], 'authors': list(d['authors']),
74	            'venue': d['venue'], 'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article',
75	            'fulltext': fulltext, 'abstract': ''}
76	
77	
78	def _four_manifestation_graph(d):
79	    """One graph holding the four canonical manifestations for domain `d`, keyed by role.
80	
81	      unresolved   -> UNRESOLVED_BINDING  (complete, but self-identifies nowhere)
82	      different    -> DIFFERENT_WORK       (foreign front-matter DOI)
83	      preprint     -> VERSION_OF_PREPRINT  (self-identifies + preprint furniture)
84	      journal      -> VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED (self-identifies + journal furniture)
85	    """
86	    rows = [
87	        _row(d, fulltext=(f'1. Introduction\n{NEUTRAL}\n4. Results\n'
88	                          'We estimate the effect is 0.2 units.\n')),        # unresolved
89	        _row(d, fulltext=(f'{d["title"]}\n{d["byline"]}\n'
90	                          f'{JOURNAL_MASTHEAD} doi: {d["foreign_doi"]}\n'
91	                          f'1. Introduction\n{FILLER}\n4. Results\n'
92	                          'We estimate the effect is 0.2 units.\n')),         # different work
93	        _row(d, fulltext=_scholarly_body(d, furniture=PREPRINT_STAMP)),       # preprint
94	        _row(d, fulltext=_scholarly_body(d, furniture='')),                   # journal
95	    ]
96	    g = P.migrate(rows)
97	    # migrate preserves order; recover the four manifestations positionally, then verify the LIVE
98	    # semantic binding each one derived, so the roles are asserted from the bytes, not assumed.
99	    mans = list(g.manifestations.values())
100	    assert len(mans) == 4, mans
101	    roles = dict(zip(('unresolved', 'different', 'preprint', 'journal'), mans))
102	    assert roles['unresolved'].profile['semantic_binding'] == 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
103	    assert roles['different'].profile['semantic_binding'] == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
104	    assert roles['preprint'].profile['semantic_binding'] == 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'
105	    assert roles['journal'].profile['semantic_binding'] == 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED'
106	    return g, roles
107	
108	
109	# ══ THE FOUR IDENTITY FAILURES ARE PRE-SKIPPED INTO SEPARATE BUCKETS; THE TWO GOOD ONES SURVIVE ═════
110	
111	@pytest.mark.parametrize('d', DOMAINS, ids=[d['id'] for d in DOMAINS])
112	@pytest.mark.parametrize('policy', [ANY_VERSION, JOURNAL_ONLY], ids=['any', 'journal_only'])
113	def test_preskip_selection_and_buckets(d, policy):
114	    g, roles = _four_manifestation_graph(d)
115	    units, skipped = EM._mining_units(g, [], policy)
116	
117	    unit_ids = {u['manifestation_id'] for u in units}
118	
119	    # unresolved and different work are ABSENT from mining units...
120	    assert roles['unresolved'].id not in unit_ids
121	    assert roles['different'].id not in unit_ids
122	    # ...and counted in SEPARATE buckets under the exact Sol names.
123	    assert {u['manifestation_id'] for u in skipped.get('identity_unresolved_lead', [])} \
124	        == {roles['unresolved'].id}
125	    assert {u['manifestation_id'] for u in skipped.get('different_work_quarantine', [])} \
126	        == {roles['different'].id}
127	
128	    # the journal remains available for mining under BOTH policies...
129	    assert roles['journal'].id in unit_ids
130	    # ...and so does the preprint: VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED is NOT pre-skipped, because a verified
131	    # exact-span correspondence may yet name a permitted expression for one of its spans.
132	    assert roles['preprint'].id in unit_ids
133	
134	    # the detail entries carry the required structured fields, not prose the caller must parse.
135	    for entry in skipped['identity_unresolved_lead'] + skipped['different_work_quarantine']:
136	        assert set(entry) >= {'manifestation_id', 'work_id', 'content_hash', 'identity_verdict',
137	                              'disposition', 'reason_code', 'why'}
138	    assert skipped['identity_unresolved_lead'][0]['reason_code'] == RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED
139	    assert skipped['different_work_quarantine'][0]['reason_code'] == RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK
140	    assert skipped['identity_unresolved_lead'][0]['identity_verdict'] == 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
141	    assert skipped['different_work_quarantine'][0]['identity_verdict'] == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
142	
143	
144	# ══ THE PRE-SKIPPED MANIFESTATIONS NEVER REACH THE LLM/MINER ═══════════════════════════════════════
145	
146	@pytest.mark.parametrize('policy', [ANY_VERSION, JOURNAL_ONLY], ids=['any', 'journal_only'])
147	def test_llm_never_called_for_preskipped(tmp_path, monkeypatch, policy):
148	    d = DOMAINS[0]
149	    g, roles = _four_manifestation_graph(d)
150	
151	    # A corpus file is required by mine(); its content is irrelevant here because we pass the graph.
152	    corpus_path = tmp_path / 'corpus.json'
153	    corpus_path.write_text(json.dumps([]))
154	
155	    seen: list[str] = []
156	
157	    def _spy_mine_paper(paper, *a, **kw):
158	        seen.append(paper['manifestation_id'])
159	        return []          # the actual LLM/harvest machinery below this point is never exercised
160	
161	    monkeypatch.setattr(EM, 'mine_paper', _spy_mine_paper)
162	
163	    EM.mine(corpus_path, question='', use_llm=True, graph=g, source_policy=policy)
164	
165	    # the miner is invoked ONLY for the two admissible/available manifestations...
166	    assert roles['journal'].id in seen
167	    assert roles['preprint'].id in seen
168	    # ...and NEVER for the pre-skipped identity failures — no spend on bytes that can never be cited.
169	    assert roles['unresolved'].id not in seen
170	    assert roles['different'].id not in seen
171	
172	
173	# ══ UNKNOWN VERDICT AND DERIVATION CONFLICT GET THEIR OWN BUCKETS (in-memory corruption) ═══════════
174	
175	def test_unknown_verdict_and_conflict_buckets():
176	    """The remaining two buckets fire on the structured reason_code the resolver emits for a tampered /
177	    corrupted in-memory graph. We corrupt AFTER a clean derive, exactly as an in-RAM bit-flip would, and
178	    assert the selector routes each to its own bucket without touching the good manifestations."""
179	    d = DOMAINS[0]
180	    g, roles = _four_manifestation_graph(d)
181	
182	    # (a) an unknown semantic binding on the (previously) journal manifestation -> UNKNOWN_VERDICT.
183	    roles['journal'].profile['semantic_binding'] = 'SAMEISH_WORK'
184	    # (b) relabel the preprint's own expression node to a journal version without changing its bytes:
185	    #     the reducer still says VERSION_OF_PREPRINT, so the pair is impossible -> DERIVATION_CONFLICT.
186	    g.expressions[roles['preprint'].expression_id].kind = 'journal_version'
187	
188	    units, skipped = EM._mining_units(g, [], ANY_VERSION)
189	    unit_ids = {u['manifestation_id'] for u in units}
190	
191	    assert {u['manifestation_id'] for u in skipped.get('identity_integrity_quarantine', [])} \
192	        == {roles['journal'].id}
193	    assert {u['manifestation_id'] for u in skipped.get('derivation_conflict_quarantine', [])} \
194	        == {roles['preprint'].id}
195	    assert skipped['identity_integrity_quarantine'][0]['reason_code'] == RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT
196	    assert skipped['derivation_conflict_quarantine'][0]['reason_code'] == RC_DERIVATION_CONFLICT
197	    # the untouched unresolved/different manifestations are still routed to THEIR buckets, not these.
198	    assert roles['journal'].id not in unit_ids
199	    assert roles['preprint'].id not in unit_ids
200	
201	
202	# ══ METAMORPHIC: identical STRUCTURE across four unrelated domains => identical selection & counts ══
203	
204	def test_metamorphic_counts_invariant_across_domains():
205	    """Replace every DOI, title, author, venue, and subject term across four unrelated fields. Because
206	    the selector keys on typed structure alone, the bucket counts and the surviving-unit count must be
207	    byte-for-byte identical for all of them — proof the P4 optimization is not tuned to any subject."""
208	    signatures = set()
209	    for d in DOMAINS:
210	        g, roles = _four_manifestation_graph(d)
211	        units, skipped = EM._mining_units(g, [], ANY_VERSION)
212	        sig = (len(units),
213	               len(skipped.get('identity_unresolved_lead', [])),
214	               len(skipped.get('different_work_quarantine', [])),
215	               len(skipped.get('identity_integrity_quarantine', [])),
216	               len(skipped.get('derivation_conflict_quarantine', [])))
217	        signatures.add(sig)
218	    assert len(signatures) == 1, signatures
219	    # the one signature is the expected shape: 2 survive (preprint+journal), 1 unresolved, 1 different.
220	    assert signatures == {(2, 1, 1, 0, 0)}
221

content.text: Now the P5 salvage test and the P1S source policy test.

content.content:
1	"""P5 — positive machine-metadata salvage for unresolved manifestations.
2	
3	Every test drives the REAL chain: ingest bytes that derive UNRESOLVED_BINDING, run the salvage
4	reducer over the raw artifact, and check that identity is promoted ONLY on positive, revalidatable
5	self-metadata — and never on a stranger's DOI, a body mention, a generic title, or a conflicting
6	self-identifier. Loader revalidation and OCR refusal are exercised end-to-end through to_json/from_json.
7	
8	No production code branches on any subject/domain label: the metamorphic test proves the outcome is
9	determined by structure by swapping four unrelated Work identities and changing only the identifier.
10	"""
11	import hashlib
12	import os
13	import sys
14	from pathlib import Path
15	
16	import pytest
17	
18	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
19	sys.path.insert(0, str(_ROOT / 'scripts'))
20	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent / 'fixtures' / 'identity_metadata'))
21	
22	import identity_receipts as ir           # noqa: E402
23	import provenance as P                    # noqa: E402
24	from acquisition import BlobStore         # noqa: E402
25	from event_ledger import IDENTITY_PROVEN, UNRESOLVED  # noqa: E402
26	import build_fixtures as F                # noqa: E402
27	
28	
29	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
30	# helpers
31	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
32	
33	_CT = {'pdf': 'application/pdf', 'html': 'text/html', 'jats': 'application/xml'}
34	
35	
36	def _salvage(raw, work, media):
37	    return ir.build_salvage(
38	        raw, work, manifestation_id='m', manifestation_content_hash='ch',
39	        artifact_blob_id='sha256:' + hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest(),
40	        artifact_sha256=hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest(), media_type=media)
41	
42	
43	def _build_unresolved_graph(work_obj, raw, media, blobs):
44	    """Ingest a generic body (-> UNRESOLVED) with raw-artifact lineage into a fresh graph."""
45	    g = P.Graph()
46	    w, claimed_id, claimed_kind = P.ensure_work(
47	        g, doi=work_obj.doi or '', title=work_obj.title, authors=list(work_obj.authors),
48	        year=work_obj.year, venue=work_obj.venue or '', source_type='journal')
49	    blob_id, sha = blobs.put(raw)
50	    mid = P.ingest_bytes(
51	        g, w, F.GENERIC_BODY, text_field='fulltext', fetched_by='fixture',
52	        locator=None, locator_status='NOT_RECORDED_BY_FETCHER',
53	        claimed_id=claimed_id, claimed_kind=claimed_kind,
54	        raw_blob_id=blob_id, raw_content_hash=sha, content_type=_CT[media])
55	    return g, mid
56	
57	
58	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
59	# 1. The six admissible containers promote; the four non-promoting shapes do not.
60	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
61	
62	@pytest.mark.parametrize('fx', F.promoting_fixtures(), ids=lambda f: f['name'])
63	def test_six_containers_promote(fx):
64	    res = _salvage(fx['raw'], fx['work'], fx['media'])
65	    assert res.conflict == '', f'{fx["name"]} unexpectedly conflicted: {res.basis}'
66	    assert res.receipts, f'{fx["name"]} produced no receipt: {res.basis}'
67	    # a verified receipt, fed to the reducer, promotes identity out of UNRESOLVED
68	    from event_ledger import observe_text, derive_binding_core
69	    sup = ir.receipts_supplement(res.receipts)
70	    prof = {**observe_text(F.GENERIC_BODY), **sup}
71	    verdict, _ = derive_binding_core(
72	        requested_doi=fx['work'].doi or '', requested_title=fx['work'].title,
73	        requested_authors=list(fx['work'].authors), prof=prof)
74	    assert verdict in IDENTITY_PROVEN, f'{fx["name"]} did not promote: {verdict}'
75	    # every promoting receipt revalidates against its own raw bytes
76	    from dataclasses import asdict
77	    ok, errs = ir.revalidate_all(fx['raw'], [asdict(r) for r in res.receipts], fx['work'])
78	    assert ok, errs
79	
80	
81	@pytest.mark.parametrize('fx', F.non_promoting_fixtures(), ids=lambda f: f['name'])
82	def test_non_promoting_shapes_stay_unresolved(fx):
83	    res = _salvage(fx['raw'], fx['work'], fx['media'])
84	    if fx['expect'] == 'conflict':
85	        assert res.conflict == ir.IDENTITY_METADATA_CONFLICT
86	        assert not res.receipts
87	    else:
88	        assert res.conflict == ''
89	        assert not res.receipts, f'{fx["name"]} should not have promoted: {res.basis}'
90	
91	
92	def test_exactly_six_of_the_thirteen_promote():
93	    """The plan's headline assertion: of the thirteen fixtures, ONLY the first six promote."""
94	    promoted = sum(1 for fx in F.promoting_fixtures()
95	                   if _salvage(fx['raw'], fx['work'], fx['media']).receipts)
96	    non_promoted = sum(1 for fx in F.non_promoting_fixtures()
97	                       if _salvage(fx['raw'], fx['work'], fx['media']).receipts)
98	    assert promoted == 6
99	    assert non_promoted == 0
100	
101	
102	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
103	# 2. The full graph chain: ingest UNRESOLVED -> reresolve -> promote -> to_json -> from_json.
104	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
105	
106	def test_reresolve_promotes_and_roundtrips(tmp_path):
107	    blobs = BlobStore(tmp_path / 'blobs')
108	    fx = F.promoting_fixtures()[0]                 # PDF Info DOI
109	    g, mid = _build_unresolved_graph(fx['work'], fx['raw'], fx['media'], blobs)
110	    assert g.manifestations[mid].profile['semantic_binding'] == UNRESOLVED
111	    assert not g.manifestations[mid].identity_receipts
112	
113	    stats = P.reresolve_unresolved_metadata(g, blobs)
114	    assert stats['promotions'] == 1
115	    m = g.manifestations[mid]
116	    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] in IDENTITY_PROVEN
117	    assert m.identity_receipts, 'a promoted manifestation must carry its receipts'
118	    assert m.profile['semantic_binding_derived_by'].startswith('identity_receipts:')
119	
120	    # the promoted graph round-trips through the STRICT loader WITH the blob store
121	    js = g.to_json()
122	    g2 = P.Graph.from_json(js, blob_store=blobs)
123	    assert g2.manifestations[mid].profile['semantic_binding'] in IDENTITY_PROVEN
124	
125	    # ...and a graph carrying receipts CANNOT load without the blob store
126	    with pytest.raises(P.GraphIntegrityError):
127	        P.Graph.from_json(js)
128	
129	
130	def test_promoted_but_versionless_is_lead_only_never_admitted(tmp_path):
131	    """Promotion settles IDENTITY, not VERSION. A salvaged body with no version furniture is
132	    identity-proven but still LEAD_ONLY under every policy — it is never admitted as a journal."""
133	    blobs = BlobStore(tmp_path / 'blobs')
134	    fx = F.promoting_fixtures()[2]                 # HTML citation_doi
135	    g, mid = _build_unresolved_graph(fx['work'], fx['raw'], fx['media'], blobs)
136	    P.reresolve_unresolved_metadata(g, blobs)
137	    for policy in (P.ANY_VERSION, P.JOURNAL_ONLY):
138	        att = g.resolve_attribution(mid, policy)
139	        assert att.admitted is False
140	        assert att.names_expression_id is None
141	        # identity IS proven now (no longer a stranger / unresolved); it fails downstream on version or
142	        # completeness, never on an identity-fabrication verdict.
143	        assert att.identity_verdict in IDENTITY_PROVEN
144	        assert att.reason_code != P.RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK
145	        assert att.reason_code != P.RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED
146	
147	
148	def test_conflict_leaves_unresolved_and_unpromoted(tmp_path):
149	    blobs = BlobStore(tmp_path / 'blobs')
150	    fx = next(f for f in F.non_promoting_fixtures() if f['expect'] == 'conflict')
151	    g, mid = _build_unresolved_graph(fx['work'], fx['raw'], fx['media'], blobs)
152	    stats = P.reresolve_unresolved_metadata(g, blobs)
153	    assert stats['promotions'] == 0
154	    assert stats['conflicts'] == 1
155	    m = g.manifestations[mid]
156	    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == UNRESOLVED
157	    assert m.profile.get('identity_metadata') == ir.IDENTITY_METADATA_CONFLICT
158	    assert not m.identity_receipts
159	    # a conflicting manifestation resolves to a lead, never an admission
160	    assert g.resolve_attribution(mid, P.ANY_VERSION).admitted is False
161	
162	
163	def test_no_raw_artifact_cannot_be_salvaged(tmp_path):
164	    """A manifestation with no raw blob (legacy row, abstract-only) stays a residual lead."""
165	    blobs = BlobStore(tmp_path / 'blobs')
166	    g = P.Graph()
167	    w, cid, ck = P.ensure_work(g, doi='10.1/none', title='A Distinctive Untraceable Study',
168	                               authors=['Ghost'], year=2020, venue='J', source_type='journal')
169	    mid = P.ingest_bytes(g, w, F.GENERIC_BODY, text_field='fulltext', fetched_by='x',
170	                         locator=None, locator_status='NOT_RECORDED_BY_FETCHER',
171	                         claimed_id=cid, claimed_kind=ck)   # NO raw lineage
172	    stats = P.reresolve_unresolved_metadata(g, blobs)
173	    assert stats['promotions'] == 0
174	    assert stats['no_raw_artifact'] == 1
175	    assert g.manifestations[mid].profile['semantic_binding'] == UNRESOLVED
176	
177	
178	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
179	# 3. Loader refuses tampered raw artifacts, tampered offsets, and unsupported (OCR) receipts.
180	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
181	
182	def _promoted_json(tmp_path):
183	    blobs = BlobStore(tmp_path / 'blobs')
184	    fx = F.promoting_fixtures()[2]                 # HTML citation_doi
185	    g, mid = _build_unresolved_graph(fx['work'], fx['raw'], fx['media'], blobs)
186	    P.reresolve_unresolved_metadata(g, blobs)
187	    return g.to_json(), blobs, mid
188	
189	
190	def _manifest(js, mid):
191	    return next(m for m in js['manifestations'] if m['id'] == mid)
192	
193	
194	def test_loader_refuses_tampered_receipt_offsets(tmp_path):
195	    js, blobs, mid = _promoted_json(tmp_path)
196	    rec = _manifest(js, mid)['identity_receipts'][0]
197	    rec['start_offset'], rec['end_offset'] = rec['start_offset'] + 3, rec['end_offset'] + 3
198	    with pytest.raises(P.GraphIntegrityError):
199	        P.Graph.from_json(js, blob_store=blobs)
200	
201	
202	def test_loader_refuses_tampered_raw_artifact(tmp_path):
203	    js, blobs, mid = _promoted_json(tmp_path)
204	    # point the manifestation at a DIFFERENT raw artifact (one with no self-DOI at all)
205	    benign = F.html_head([('citation_title', 'Nothing Here')])
206	    bid, bsha = blobs.put(benign)
207	    m = _manifest(js, mid)
208	    m['raw_blob_id'], m['raw_content_hash'] = bid, bsha
209	    with pytest.raises(P.GraphIntegrityError):
210	        P.Graph.from_json(js, blob_store=blobs)
211	
212	
213	def test_loader_refuses_unsupported_ocr_receipt(tmp_path):
214	    js, blobs, mid = _promoted_json(tmp_path)
215	    rec = _manifest(js, mid)['identity_receipts'][0]
216	    rec['metadata_container'] = 'ocr'              # not a supported, revalidatable container
217	    rec['receipt_kind'] = 'IMAGE_OCR'
218	    with pytest.raises(P.GraphIntegrityError):
219	        P.Graph.from_json(js, blob_store=blobs)
220	
221	
222	def test_ocr_receipt_is_refused_by_revalidator_directly():
223	    """An OCR-typed receipt supplied through JSON must be refused as an unsupported receipt type."""
224	    ok, why = ir.revalidate_receipt(b'', {'metadata_container': 'ocr', 'receipt_kind': 'IMAGE_OCR',
225	                                          'extractor_name': ir.EXTRACTOR_NAME,
226	                                          'extractor_version': ir.EXTRACTOR_VERSION}, F.DOMAINS['cs'])
227	    assert ok is False
228	    assert 'ocr' in why.lower() or 'unsupported' in why.lower()
229	
230	
231	def test_loader_refuses_forged_promotion_without_receipt(tmp_path):
232	    """The salvage superhighway attack: hand-edit a stored SAME_WORK onto UNRESOLVED bytes with NO
233	    receipt to back it. The loader re-derives from bytes and refuses the promotion."""
234	    blobs = BlobStore(tmp_path / 'blobs')
235	    fx = F.promoting_fixtures()[2]
236	    g, mid = _build_unresolved_graph(fx['work'], fx['raw'], fx['media'], blobs)
237	    js = g.to_json()
238	    m = _manifest(js, mid)
239	    m['profile']['semantic_binding'] = 'SAME_WORK'   # forged, no receipt supports it
240	    with pytest.raises(P.GraphIntegrityError):
241	        P.Graph.from_json(js, blob_store=blobs)
242	
243	
244	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
245	# 4. Metamorphic: the outcome is structural. Swap four unrelated identities; change only the DOI.
246	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
247	
248	@pytest.mark.parametrize('domain', list(F.DOMAINS))
249	def test_metamorphic_identifier_swap(domain):
250	    work = F.DOMAINS[domain]
251	    raw = F.html_head([('citation_doi', work.doi)])
252	
253	    # extraction behavior is a property of the CONTAINER, not the identity
254	    obs = ir.extract_field_observations(raw, 'html')
255	    doi_fields = [o for o in obs if o['field'] == ir.FIELD_DOI]
256	    assert len(doi_fields) == 1
257	
258	    # with the matching identity -> promotes
259	    good = _salvage(raw, work, 'html')
260	    assert good.receipts and not good.conflict
261	
262	    # change ONLY the requested DOI (same artifact) -> the self-DOI is now foreign -> no promotion.
263	    # Extraction is byte-identical; only the comparison to the request changed.
264	    from dataclasses import replace as _dc_replace
265	    mutated = _dc_replace(work, doi='10.0000/unrelated.' + domain)
266	    bad = _salvage(raw, work=mutated, media='html')
267	    assert not bad.receipts
268	    assert bad.conflict == ir.IDENTITY_METADATA_CONFLICT
269	    assert len(ir.extract_field_observations(raw, 'html')) == len(obs)  # extraction unchanged
270	
271	
272	def test_no_production_module_branches_on_domain_labels():
273	    """The generality invariant: no domain vocabulary decides anything in the salvage code."""
274	    src = (Path(F.__file__).parent.parent.parent.parent / 'scripts' / 'identity_receipts.py').read_text()
275	    for token in ('medicine', 'sepsis', 'tort', 'wage', 'attention', 'law review', 'clinical'):
276	        assert token not in src.lower(), f'salvage code must not name the subject {token!r}'
277	
278	
279	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
280	# 5. Cohort gate — the recorded-corpus regression (opt-in: needs the full ledger + raw blob store).
281	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
282	
283	@pytest.mark.skipif(os.environ.get('POLARIS_RUN_COHORT') != '1',
284	                    reason='cohort regression needs the full corpus + raw blobs; set POLARIS_RUN_COHORT=1')
285	def test_cohort_regression():
286	    import json
287	    from acquisition import open_ledger
288	    from provenance_construct import construct
289	    ledger = open_ledger()
290	    blobs = BlobStore()
291	    biblio_p = _ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
292	    biblio = json.loads(biblio_p.read_text()) if biblio_p.exists() else None
293	    g, _ = construct(ledger, blobs=blobs, bibliography=biblio)
294	
295	    baseline = [mid for mid, m in g.manifestations.items()
296	                if m.profile.get('semantic_binding') == UNRESOLVED]
297	    stats = P.reresolve_unresolved_metadata(g, blobs)
298	
299	    # ── THE CORPUS-INVARIANT SAFETY PROPERTIES (these must hold at ANY corpus size) ──────────────
300	    # 1. zero residual unresolved manifestation is admitted under either policy — no fabrication.
301	    for mid, m in g.manifestations.items():
302	        if m.profile.get('semantic_binding') == UNRESOLVED:
303	            assert g.resolve_attribution(mid, P.ANY_VERSION).admitted is False
304	            assert g.resolve_attribution(mid, P.JOURNAL_ONLY).admitted is False
305	    # 2. every promotion carries a receipt that revalidates against its raw artifact.
306	    for mid in stats['promoted_ids']:
307	        m = g.manifestations[mid]
308	        assert m.identity_receipts
309	        raw = blobs.get(m.raw_blob_id)
310	        ok, errs = ir.revalidate_all(raw, m.identity_receipts, g.works[m.work_id])
311	        assert ok, errs
312	    # 3. residual == initial - promotions; no OCR promotion; promotions never exceed the baseline.
313	    assert stats['residual'] == len(baseline) - stats['promotions']
314	    assert 0 <= stats['promotions'] <= len(baseline)
315	    for mid in stats['promoted_ids']:
316	        for r in g.manifestations[mid].identity_receipts:
317	            assert r['metadata_container'] in ir.SUPPORTED_CONTAINERS   # never OCR
318	
319	    # The audit count (~67 against Sol's recorded 155-unresolved snapshot) is a corpus-state
320	    # OBSERVATION, not a production threshold (Sol global rule 7). It is reported, never asserted as a
321	    # magic number: the live ledger has since resolved most of that cohort, so the invariants above —
322	    # not the count — are what the gate enforces.
323	    print(f'\nCOHORT: initial unresolved={len(baseline)} promotions={stats["promotions"]} '
324	          f'conflicts={stats["conflicts"]} residual={stats["residual"]} '
325	          f'no_raw={stats["no_raw_artifact"]}')
326

1	"""P1S — the adjustable source policy is INFERRED FROM THE ORIGINAL PROMPT.
2	
3	The same valid, complete, identity-proven PREPRINT fixture runs through the REAL chain
4	(migrate/ingest -> the one identity reducer -> Graph.resolve_attribution(binding, policy)). What
5	changes across cases is ONLY the prompt, and therefore only the policy `research_contract.
6	derive_version_scope` infers from it. Nothing is mocked and no verdict is hand-assigned.
7	
8	Generality (Sol global rules 1 & 6): `derive_version_scope` keys on source-class + directive
9	structure ONLY — never on a DOI, title, author, venue, or subject literal. The metamorphic test
10	replaces the subject with unrelated clinical, legal, economics, and CS vocabulary and asserts the
11	inferred policy is unchanged.
12	"""
13	import pytest
14	
15	import provenance as P
16	import research_contract as rc
17	from provenance import DISPOSITION_ADMIT, DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY, RC_ADMITTED, RC_VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED
18	
19	FILLER = ('This paper studies the question at length across many pages of careful analysis, '
20	          'reporting the design, the data and the estimates in full. ') * 110
21	
22	
23	def _preprint_row(requested_doi='10.2222/bbbb',
24	                  title='A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism',
25	                  byline='By Alice Adams and Bob Brown', authors=('Adams', 'Brown')):
26	    """A COMPLETE, IDENTITY-PROVEN WORKING PAPER: the article's own front matter carries the requested
27	    DOI (so the bytes are the requested work) AND a working-paper stamp (so the version is a preprint,
28	    never a journal article). One fixture; the policy is the only thing that moves."""
29	    src = (f'{title}\nNBER Working Paper No. 26123\ndoi: {requested_doi}\n{byline}\n'
30	           f'1. Introduction\n{FILLER}\n4. Results\n'
31	           f'We find the effect is 0.2 units (standard error 0.05) across 722 sites.\n')
32	    return {'doi': requested_doi, 'title': title, 'authors': list(authors),
33	            'venue': 'Journal of Widgets', 'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article',
34	            'fulltext': src, 'abstract': ''}
35	
36	
37	def _resolve_under(prompt, row=None):
38	    """Infer the policy FROM THE PROMPT, then resolve the fixture under it. Returns (policy, att)."""
39	    scope, _ev = rc.derive_version_scope(prompt)
40	    policy = P.JOURNAL_ONLY if scope == 'JOURNAL_ONLY' else P.ANY_VERSION
41	    g = P.migrate([row or _preprint_row()])
42	    m = next(iter(g.manifestations.values()))
43	    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'  # the fixture really is a preprint
44	    return policy, g.resolve_attribution(m.id, policy)
45	
46	
47	# ── the fixture is genuinely a valid, complete preprint ──────────────────────────────────────────
48	
49	def test_fixture_is_a_complete_identity_proven_preprint():
50	    g = P.migrate([_preprint_row()])
51	    m = next(iter(g.manifestations.values()))
52	    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'
53	    assert g.expressions[m.expression_id].kind == 'working_paper'
54	
55	
56	# ── THE ACCEPTANCE TABLE: same bytes, prompt decides the policy ──────────────────────────────────
57	
58	ANY_VERSION_PROMPTS = [
59	    'Summarize the evidence about X.',
60	    'Use journal articles and preprints about X.',
61	    'Explain differences between journals and preprint servers.',
62	    'Do not limit this to journal articles; include working papers.',
63	]
64	
65	JOURNAL_ONLY_PROMPTS = [
66	    'Use only peer-reviewed sources about X.',
67	    'Cite published journal articles about X.',
68	]
69	
70	
71	@pytest.mark.parametrize('prompt', ANY_VERSION_PROMPTS)
72	def test_any_version_prompts_admit_the_preprint(prompt):
73	    policy, att = _resolve_under(prompt)
74	    assert policy is P.ANY_VERSION, f'{prompt!r} should infer ANY_VERSION'
75	    assert att.admitted is True
76	    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_ADMIT
77	    assert att.reason_code == RC_ADMITTED
78	    assert att.names_expression_id is not None
79	    assert att.names_expression_id.endswith('working_paper')  # names the PREPRINT, never a journal
80	
81	
82	@pytest.mark.parametrize('prompt', JOURNAL_ONLY_PROMPTS)
83	def test_journal_only_prompts_reduce_the_preprint_to_a_lead(prompt):
84	    policy, att = _resolve_under(prompt)
85	    assert policy is P.JOURNAL_ONLY, f'{prompt!r} should infer JOURNAL_ONLY'
86	    # THE EXACT REFUSAL CONTRACT the plan requires.
87	    assert att.admitted is False
88	    assert att.disposition == 'LEAD_ONLY'
89	    assert att.reason_code == 'VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED'
90	    assert att.names_expression_id is None
91	    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY
92	    assert att.reason_code == RC_VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED
93	
94	
95	# ── POSITIVE PROOF ONLY: discussion / negation / widening never set the gate ─────────────────────
96	
97	def test_widening_clause_defeats_the_journal_class_demand():
98	    # A source-class phrase AND a directive, but widened in the SAME clause -> ANY_VERSION.
99	    scope, ev = rc.derive_version_scope('Use journal articles and preprints about labour markets.')
100	    assert scope == 'ANY_VERSION' and ev == []
101	
102	
103	def test_negation_clause_defeats_the_journal_class_demand():
104	    scope, ev = rc.derive_version_scope('Do not restrict to journal articles about the topic.')
105	    assert scope == 'ANY_VERSION' and ev == []
106	
107	
108	def test_mere_comparison_of_source_types_does_not_set_the_gate():
109	    scope, ev = rc.derive_version_scope('Contrast journals and preprint servers as venues.')
110	    assert scope == 'ANY_VERSION' and ev == []
111	
112	
113	def test_positive_directive_returns_the_verbatim_clause():
114	    q = 'First, gather the data. Then use only peer-reviewed sources about wage inequality.'
115	    scope, ev = rc.derive_version_scope(q)
116	    assert scope == 'JOURNAL_ONLY'
117	    assert ev == ['Then use only peer-reviewed sources about wage inequality']
118	    for clause in ev:                       # every returned clause is a VERBATIM span of the prompt
119	        assert clause in q
120	
121	
122	# ── the two SourcePolicy fields are wired and non-negotiable through the compiler ────────────────
123	
124	def test_floor_source_policy_sets_the_version_scope_fields():
125	    q = 'Use only peer-reviewed sources about diabetes.'
126	    sp = rc._floor_source_policy(q)
127	    assert sp.version_scope == 'JOURNAL_ONLY'
128	    assert sp.version_scope_evidence
129	    assert all(clause in q for clause in sp.version_scope_evidence)   # verbatim spans of the prompt
130	    sp2 = rc._floor_source_policy('Summarize what is known about diabetes.')
131	    assert sp2.version_scope == 'ANY_VERSION'
132	    assert sp2.version_scope_evidence == []
133	
134	
135	def test_offline_contract_carries_the_prompt_derived_version_scope():
136	    c = rc.compile_contract('Cite published journal articles about hypertension.',
137	                            use_llm=False, verbose=False, force=True)
138	    assert c.source_policy.version_scope == 'JOURNAL_ONLY'
139	    c2 = rc.compile_contract('Review what is known about hypertension.',
140	                             use_llm=False, verbose=False, force=True)
141	    assert c2.source_policy.version_scope == 'ANY_VERSION'
142	
143	
144	# ── the event-ledger eligibility lane consumes the SAME policy ───────────────────────────────────
145	
146	def test_event_ledger_eligibility_consults_the_policy():
147	    import event_ledger as EL
148	    L = EL.Ledger()
149	    src = _preprint_row()['fulltext']
150	    L.emit('u', EL.EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, 'test', adapter='x', locator='u',
151	           blob_id='sha256:b', byte_sha256='b', text_blob_id='sha256:t', text_sha256='t',
152	           requested_title='A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism',
153	           requested_authors=['Adams', 'Brown'], requested_doi='10.2222/bbbb',
154	           source_type='journal-article')
155	    L.emit('u', EL.EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED, 'observe_text', **EL.observe_text(src))
156	    # Default ANY_VERSION: a proven preprint is ADMISSIBLE as its own expression, NOT a lead.
157	    elig_any, _ = EL.derive_eligibility(L.events('u'))
158	    assert elig_any == EL.ADMISSIBLE
159	    # JOURNAL_ONLY: the same binding is a discovery lead — the policy does not permit its kind.
160	    elig_jo, _ = EL.derive_eligibility(L.events('u'), policy=P.JOURNAL_ONLY)
161	    assert elig_jo == EL.DISCOVERY_LEAD
162	
163	
164	# ── METAMORPHIC: replace the subject; the inferred policy must not move ───────────────────────────
165	
166	_SUBJECTS = ['type 2 diabetes remission', 'qualified immunity doctrine',
167	             'minimum wage employment effects', 'transformer inference latency']
168	
169	
170	@pytest.mark.parametrize('subject', _SUBJECTS)
171	def test_journal_only_is_subject_invariant(subject):
172	    scope, _ev = rc.derive_version_scope(f'Use only peer-reviewed sources about {subject}.')
173	    assert scope == 'JOURNAL_ONLY'
174	
175	
176	@pytest.mark.parametrize('subject', _SUBJECTS)
177	def test_any_version_is_subject_invariant(subject):
178	    scope, _ev = rc.derive_version_scope(f'Summarize the current evidence about {subject}.')
179	    assert scope == 'ANY_VERSION'
180	
181	
182	@pytest.mark.parametrize('subject', _SUBJECTS)
183	def test_full_chain_admission_is_subject_invariant(subject):
184	    # Change the fixture's subject/identifiers AND the prompt subject together; structure is held.
185	    doi = '10.5555/' + str(abs(hash(subject)) % 100000)
186	    row = _preprint_row(requested_doi=doi, title=f'A Structured Study of {subject}',
187	                        byline='By Casey Doe and Dana Roe', authors=('Doe', 'Roe'))
188	    _, att_any = _resolve_under(f'Summarize the evidence about {subject}.', row=row)
189	    assert att_any.admitted is True and att_any.names_expression_id.endswith('working_paper')
190	    _, att_jo = _resolve_under(f'Use only peer-reviewed sources about {subject}.', row=row)
191	    assert att_jo.admitted is False and att_jo.reason_code == 'VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED'
192	
193	
194	# ── policy laundering is refused: an explicit override that disagrees with the prompt raises ──────
195	
196	def test_mine_refuses_an_explicit_policy_that_disagrees_with_the_prompt(tmp_path):
197	    import json
198	    import evidence_miner as EM
199	    corpus = tmp_path / 'corpus.json'
200	    corpus.write_text(json.dumps([_preprint_row()]))
201	    # The prompt demands journal-only; passing ANY_VERSION is a laundering attempt and must raise.
202	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
203	        EM.mine(corpus, question='Use only peer-reviewed sources about the widget mechanism.',
204	                use_llm=False, source_policy=P.ANY_VERSION)

content.text: Now the P3 correspondence test and the P5 fixture builder.

content.content:
1	"""P3 — correspondence and whole-copy edges must authorize on SEMANTIC IDENTITY, not the legacy
2	whole-body `identity.verdict`.
3	
4	The disease this phase kills: the old `identity_agreement()` verdict ("CONFIRMED") scans the WHOLE
5	document, so it "confirms" any requested author it finds ANYWHERE — including in the references. A
6	manuscript that merely CITES the requested authors would score CONFIRMED, and the three correspondence
7	seams (`make_correspondence`, `Graph.exact_copy_failure`, `Graph.verify_correspondence`) trusted that
8	verdict. After P3 those seams re-derive the SEMANTIC BINDING from the bytes and the Work through the ONE
9	reducer, admit only `IDENTITY_PROVEN` verdicts, and reject a stale stored value.
10	
11	Every fixture runs through the REAL chain: migrate()/ingest_bytes -> event_ledger.derive_binding_core.
12	No Attribution is mocked and no successful verdict is hand-assigned; the tests only stamp the LEGACY
13	`identity.verdict` to CONFIRMED to prove that the legacy layer cannot authorize anything.
14	
15	Generality (Sol global rules 1 & 6): no rule under test keys on a DOI, title, author, venue, or subject
16	literal. The scholarly, legal, and registry variants are run over the SAME structure with every
17	identifier and subject term replaced; the structural result is identical in every case.
18	"""
19	import copy
20	
21	import pytest
22	
23	import provenance as P
24	from event_ledger import IDENTITY_PROVEN
25	
26	
27	# A span with DIGITS — canonicalization never touches them, so an exactly-shared span is exactly equal
28	# in both documents and a changed one is not (the Acemoglu test). Nothing here is a subject literal any
29	# rule reads; it is body prose the two documents happen to share.
30	SPAN = ('The estimated elasticity was 0.42 with a robust standard error of 0.06 across every '
31	        'specification we ran, and the point estimate did not move when controls were added.')
32	
33	# A long neutral header (>= 10 readable words, no DOI, no byline cue, none of any requested title word).
34	# It fills the bounded identity window so that anything in the body/references is OUT of view.
35	_NEUTRAL_HEADER = ('Section one introduces the general setting and the notation carried throughout, '
36	                   'describing the framework in broad terms before any numeric result is presented. ') * 9
37	# Body padding that pushes the shared span and the references well past the 1500-char identity window.
38	_PAD = ('The work proceeds in several stages across many regions and time periods, laying out the '
39	        'design, the collected data, and the resulting estimates in complete detail. ') * 22
40	
41	
42	def _text(header_extra: str, refs_names: str) -> str:
43	    """One document. `header_extra` is any self-identifying furniture we CHOOSE to put in the front
44	    matter (a DOI line, a title, a byline) — empty for the unresolved case. `refs_names` are author
45	    names dropped in a references block, which is PAST the identity window and must never confirm."""
46	    head = _NEUTRAL_HEADER + (header_extra + ' ' if header_extra else '')
47	    body = _PAD + ' ' + SPAN + ' References. ' + refs_names
48	    return head + body
49	
50	
51	def _graph(rows):
52	    g = P.migrate(rows)
53	    mans = list(g.manifestations.values())
54	    return g, mans
55	
56	
57	def _stamp_legacy_confirmed(m):
58	    """Make the LEGACY whole-body identity say CONFIRMED — the precondition of every 'both fail' test.
59	    If the legacy verdict could authorize, this stamp would let it. It must not."""
60	    ident = dict(m.profile.get('identity') or {})
61	    ident['verdict'] = 'CONFIRMED'
62	    ident['basis'] = 'test fixture: legacy whole-body identity stamped CONFIRMED'
63	    m.profile['identity'] = ident
64	
65	
66	# ── the scholarly rows ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67	
68	def _scholarly_rows(*, doi_a, doi_b, title_a, title_b, authors_a, authors_b, prove):
69	    """Two scholarly documents that share SPAN verbatim. `prove` toggles whether each front matter
70	    self-identifies (an exact front-matter DOI -> SAME_WORK) or stays neutral (-> UNRESOLVED_BINDING).
71	    The shared SPAN bytes are IDENTICAL in both branches — only the header furniture changes."""
72	    head_a = f'doi: {doi_a}' if prove else ''
73	    head_b = f'doi: {doi_b}' if prove else ''
74	    refs_a = f'{authors_a[0]}, and {authors_a[1]}. Earlier study. Some Journal.'
75	    refs_b = f'{authors_b[0]}, and {authors_b[1]}. Earlier study. Some Journal.'
76	    row_a = {'doi': doi_a, 'title': title_a, 'authors': list(authors_a), 'venue': 'Journal of Widgets',
77	             'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article', 'fulltext': _text(head_a, refs_a), 'abstract': ''}
78	    row_b = {'doi': doi_b, 'title': title_b, 'authors': list(authors_b), 'venue': 'Journal of Gadgets',
79	             'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article', 'fulltext': _text(head_b, refs_b), 'abstract': ''}
80	    return [row_a, row_b]
81	
82	
83	_SCHOLARLY = dict(doi_a='10.1000/aaaa', doi_b='10.2000/bbbb',
84	                  title_a='A Rigorous Empirical Study of the Widget Mechanism',
85	                  title_b='A Careful Longitudinal Analysis of the Gadget Apparatus',
86	                  authors_a=('Adams', 'Brown'), authors_b=('Carter', 'Dupont'))
87	
88	
89	def _typed_rows(*, type_str, prove):
90	    """The SAME structure with two TYPED works (a court opinion / a trial registry record). Identity is
91	    proven by an exact self-title in the front matter, not by a byline or a field — the point of the
92	    metamorphic case. Every identifier and subject term differs from the scholarly rows."""
93	    title_a = 'Henderson versus Continental Freight Holdings Incorporated'
94	    title_b = 'Ferndale Municipal Authority versus Okafor Logistics Group'
95	    head_a = title_a if prove else ''
96	    head_b = title_b if prove else ''
97	    row_a = {'title': title_a, 'authors': [], 'year': 2018, 'venue': 'Court of Appeal', 'type': type_str,
98	             'fulltext': _text(head_a, 'Unrelated Reporter Citation 42.'), 'abstract': ''}
99	    row_b = {'title': title_b, 'authors': [], 'year': 2019, 'venue': 'High Court', 'type': type_str,
100	             'fulltext': _text(head_b, 'Unrelated Reporter Citation 91.'), 'abstract': ''}
101	    return [row_a, row_b]
102	
103	
104	# =================================================================================================
105	# 1. THE CORE FAILURE — legacy CONFIRMED must NOT authorize when semantic identity is unresolved
106	# =================================================================================================
107	
108	def _corr(g, src, tgt):
109	    ss = src.text.index(SPAN)
110	    ts = tgt.text.index(SPAN)
111	    return P.make_correspondence(g, src.id, ss, ss + len(SPAN), tgt.id, ts, ts + len(SPAN),
112	                                 basis='exact canonical equality of the shared span in both held bytes')
113	
114	
115	def test_unresolved_bytes_block_correspondence_even_when_legacy_says_confirmed():
116	    g, (src, tgt) = _graph(_scholarly_rows(prove=False, **_SCHOLARLY))
117	
118	    # Precondition: semantic identity is UNRESOLVED for BOTH, while legacy whole-body identity is CONFIRMED.
119	    for m in (src, tgt):
120	        assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
121	        _stamp_legacy_confirmed(m)
122	        assert m.profile['identity']['verdict'] == 'CONFIRMED'
123	
124	    # (a) the whole-document exact_copy_of edge cannot be ASSERTED.
125	    with pytest.raises(ValueError) as ei:
126	        g.add_edge(src.expression_id, tgt.expression_id, 'exact_copy_of', 'ASSERTED',
127	                   basis='byte-for-byte comparison: identical')
128	    assert 'semantic binding' in str(ei.value)
129	    why = g.exact_copy_failure(src.expression_id, tgt.expression_id)
130	    assert why and 'PROVEN' in why
131	
132	    # (b) the per-span correspondence does not verify, and add_correspondence refuses it.
133	    sc = _corr(g, src, tgt)
134	    ok, reasons = g.verify_correspondence(sc)
135	    assert ok is False
136	    assert any('semantic binding' in r for r in reasons)
137	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
138	        g.add_correspondence(sc)
139	    assert g.correspondences == []          # nothing reached the graph on the legacy verdict's word
140	
141	
142	def test_valid_semantic_identity_admits_the_same_equal_span():
143	    # SAME shared SPAN bytes, now with self-identifying front matter -> SAME_WORK for both.
144	    g, (src, tgt) = _graph(_scholarly_rows(prove=True, **_SCHOLARLY))
145	    for m in (src, tgt):
146	        assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] in IDENTITY_PROVEN
147	
148	    sc = _corr(g, src, tgt)
149	    assert sc.source_identity in IDENTITY_PROVEN and sc.target_identity in IDENTITY_PROVEN
150	    ok, reasons = g.verify_correspondence(sc)
151	    assert ok is True, reasons
152	    g.add_correspondence(sc)                # accepted — identity is proven and the span is exactly equal
153	    assert len(g.correspondences) == 1
154	
155	
156	# =================================================================================================
157	# 2. SEMANTIC BINDING, not the stored token, is authoritative
158	# =================================================================================================
159	
160	def test_stale_stored_correspondence_identity_is_rejected():
161	    g, (src, tgt) = _graph(_scholarly_rows(prove=True, **_SCHOLARLY))
162	    sc = _corr(g, src, tgt)
163	    # A JSON file could carry a correspondence whose stored identity disagrees with the live bytes.
164	    sc.source_identity = 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
165	    ok, reasons = g.verify_correspondence(sc)
166	    assert ok is False
167	    assert any('stale' in r for r in reasons)
168	
169	
170	def test_correspondence_ignores_legacy_identity_field_entirely():
171	    # Prove identity, build a valid correspondence, then POISON only the legacy whole-body verdict to a
172	    # nonsense value on both nodes. Verification must be UNAFFECTED — it never reads that field.
173	    g, (src, tgt) = _graph(_scholarly_rows(prove=True, **_SCHOLARLY))
174	    sc = _corr(g, src, tgt)
175	    for m in (src, tgt):
176	        m.profile['identity'] = {'verdict': 'DIFFERENT_WORK', 'basis': 'poisoned legacy field'}
177	    ok, reasons = g.verify_correspondence(sc)
178	    assert ok is True, reasons
179	
180	
181	def test_different_work_source_cannot_lend_a_span():
182	    # A foreign front-matter DOI makes the SOURCE a DIFFERENT_WORK; even a byte-identical span may not
183	    # cross out of a stranger's paper.
184	    rows = _scholarly_rows(prove=False, **_SCHOLARLY)
185	    rows[0]['fulltext'] = _text(f'doi: 10.9999/foreign-{"stranger"}', 'Adams, and Brown. Prior.')
186	    # keep the shared span present in the (now foreign) source
187	    assert SPAN in rows[0]['fulltext']
188	    rows[1]['fulltext'] = _text('doi: 10.2000/bbbb', 'Carter, and Dupont. Prior.')  # target proven
189	    g, (src, tgt) = _graph(rows)
190	    assert src.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
191	    sc = _corr(g, src, tgt)
192	    ok, reasons = g.verify_correspondence(sc)
193	    assert ok is False
194	    assert any('DIFFERENT_WORK' in r or 'semantic binding' in r for r in reasons)
195	
196	
197	# =================================================================================================
198	# 3. THE ACEMOGLU TEST still lives here — a non-identical span never verifies even when identity is proven
199	# =================================================================================================
200	
201	def test_changed_span_fails_at_the_bytes_under_proven_identity():
202	    g, (src, tgt) = _graph(_scholarly_rows(prove=True, **_SCHOLARLY))
203	    # A span present only in the source; the target's byte at those offsets differs. Identity is proven,
204	    # so this is a pure byte failure — exactly the peer-review-changed-a-number case.
205	    ss = src.text.index(SPAN)
206	    # bind the source's span but point the target at a DIFFERENT equal-length body slice
207	    ts = tgt.text.index(_PAD.strip()[:len(SPAN)])
208	    sc = P.make_correspondence(g, src.id, ss, ss + len(SPAN),
209	                               tgt.id, ts, ts + len(SPAN), basis='claimed equal, but it is not')
210	    ok, reasons = g.verify_correspondence(sc)
211	    assert ok is False
212	    assert any('NOT equal' in r for r in reasons)
213	
214	
215	# =================================================================================================
216	# 4. METAMORPHIC — typed legal / registry works, same structure, same result
217	# =================================================================================================
218	
219	@pytest.mark.parametrize('type_str, expect_kind', [
220	    ('case', 'case'),
221	    ('clinical-trial', 'trial'),
222	])
223	def test_typed_works_authorize_on_semantic_identity_not_field(type_str, expect_kind):
224	    # Unresolved variant: neither typed doc self-identifies -> UNRESOLVED_BINDING, correspondence fails
225	    # even with the legacy verdict stamped CONFIRMED.
226	    g, (src, tgt) = _graph(_typed_rows(type_str=type_str, prove=False))
227	    for m in (src, tgt):
228	        assert g.works[m.work_id].kind == expect_kind
229	        assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
230	        _stamp_legacy_confirmed(m)
231	    sc = _corr(g, src, tgt)
232	    ok, _ = g.verify_correspondence(sc)
233	    assert ok is False
234	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
235	        g.add_correspondence(sc)
236	
237	    # Proven variant: an exact self-title makes each typed work SAME_WORK; the SAME shared span verifies.
238	    g2, (src2, tgt2) = _graph(_typed_rows(type_str=type_str, prove=True))
239	    for m in (src2, tgt2):
240	        assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'SAME_WORK'
241	    sc2 = _corr(g2, src2, tgt2)
242	    ok2, reasons2 = g2.verify_correspondence(sc2)
243	    assert ok2 is True, reasons2
244	    g2.add_correspondence(sc2)
245	    assert len(g2.correspondences) == 1
246	
247	
248	def test_metamorphic_identifier_swap_is_structurally_invariant():
249	    # Replace every DOI/title/author/venue with a completely different set; the STRUCTURE (neutral header
250	    # -> unresolved; self-DOI -> proven) is held constant. The authorization outcome must not change.
251	    alt = dict(doi_a='10.5555/qqqq', doi_b='10.6666/rrrr',
252	               title_a='An Exhaustive Field Investigation of the Sprocket Assembly',
253	               title_b='A Systematic Review of the Flange Coupling Procedure',
254	               authors_a=('Novak', 'Ortega'), authors_b=('Pran', 'Quill'))
255	    # unresolved both ways
256	    g0, (s0, t0) = _graph(_scholarly_rows(prove=False, **alt))
257	    assert s0.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
258	    sc0 = _corr(g0, s0, t0)
259	    assert g0.verify_correspondence(sc0)[0] is False
260	    # proven both ways -> same equal span verifies
261	    g1, (s1, t1) = _graph(_scholarly_rows(prove=True, **alt))
262	    assert s1.profile['semantic_binding'] in IDENTITY_PROVEN
263	    sc1 = _corr(g1, s1, t1)
264	    assert g1.verify_correspondence(sc1)[0] is True
265	
266	
267	if __name__ == '__main__':
268	    raise SystemExit(pytest.main([__file__, '-q']))
269

README.md

580 scripts/identity_receipts.py

1 """Deterministic raw-artifact fixtures for the machine-metadata salvage tests (Sol P5). 2 3 Every fixture is a RAW artifact (PDF/HTML/JATS bytes) plus the requested identity (a tiny Work-shaped 4 object). No fixture uses the task-72 subject; identifiers and titles are invented per domain so the 5 tests can be metamorphosed across medicine/law/economics/CS by swapping the identity table alone. 6 7 The generated PDFs are built with PyMuPDF/fitz at call time — nothing binary is committed. 8 """ 9 from __future__ import annotations 10 11 import re 12 from dataclasses import dataclass, field 13 14 import fitz # PyMuPDF 15 16 17 @dataclass 18 class W: 19 """A Work-shaped identity: exactly the fields identity_receipts + derive_binding_core read.""" 20 id: str = 'work:fixture' 21 title: str = '' 22 authors: list = field(default_factory=list) 23 year: int | None = 2021 24 venue: str | None = 'Some Journal' 25 doi: str | None = None 26 kind: str = 'study' 27 28 29 #: A generic, identity-free body. Readable words, but no byline, no DOI, no title match -> UNRESOLVED 30 #: when derive_binding_core sees it alone. This is the state salvage must start from. 31 GENERIC_BODY = ('This document presents an analysis of the observed phenomena and reports the ' 32 'associated measurements across several conditions. content.text: All the self-test failures stem from one root cause: the fixtures use a requested DOI (`10.x/y`, `10.z/w`) that mismatches the DOI in the body front-matter, which the now-landed identity gate correctly derives as DIFFERENT_WORK. I'll align the requested DOIs with the body DOIs. Let me make the edits.

input.old_string:
row_j = {'doi': '10.x/y', 'title': 'Robots and Jobs: Evidence from US Labor Markets',
             'authors': ['Acemoglu', 'Restrepo'], 'venue': 'Journal of Political Economy',
             'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article', 'fulltext': JOURNAL_SRC, 'abstract': ''}
    G = build_graph([row_j])
    MID = manif_of(G, '10.x/y')
input.new_string:
# The requested DOI MATCHES the DOI the bytes self-identify with, so the identity reducer proves
    # SAME_WORK from the front matter (a mismatched requested DOI would be DIFFERENT_WORK and quarantine
    # every card built on it — the gate is doing its job, so the fixture must be an HONEST journal article).
    row_j = {'doi': '10.1086/705716', 'title': 'Robots and Jobs: Evidence from US Labor Markets',
             'authors': ['Acemoglu', 'Restrepo'], 'venue': 'Journal of Political Economy',
             'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article', 'fulltext': JOURNAL_SRC, 'abstract': ''}
    G = build_graph([row_j])
    MID = manif_of(G, '10.1086/705716')

Since bare `python scripts/provenance.py` (the acceptance command) already exits 0 via the corpus path, the acceptance itself isn't at risk — but I'll repair the embedded self-test as the plan directs, stamping the binding via the one reducer exactly as ingest does. Let me look at the opening of self_test to insert a helper.

input.new_string:
w = Work(id='work:w', title='Robots and Jobs', authors=['Acemoglu', 'Restrepo'], year=2020,
             venue='Journal of Political Economy', doi='10.1086/705716')

    # A hand-built Manifestation skips ingest_bytes, so it must carry the ONE reducer's semantic_binding
    # the resolver reads — otherwise it fails closed as IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT. `_sb` stamps exactly
    # what ingest_bytes stamps (event_ledger.derive_binding_core over the live bytes), never a hand-picked
    # verdict: these fixtures are held to the SAME positive-proof identity gate as the corpus.
    from event_ledger import (observe_text as _st_obs, derive_binding_core as _st_bind,  # noqa: PLC0415
                              version_evidence as _st_ver)

    def _sb(p, text, work=w):
        sig = _st_obs(text)
        b, ev = _st_bind(requested_doi=work.doi or '', requested_title=work.title or '',
                         requested_authors=list(work.authors or []), prof=sig,
                         work_kind=work.kind or 'study')
        p['semantic_binding'] = b
        p['semantic_binding_basis'] = ev
        p['semantic_binding_derived_by'] = 'self_test:derive_binding_core'
        return p

    g = Graph(works={'work:w': w})

       p['artifact_kind'] == 'extraction_failure' and not p['complete'])
    g.manifestations['manif:bad'] = Manifestation(
        'manif:bad', 'work:w:journal_version', 'work:w', caesar,

    # round-trip below, which asserts that a legitimate graph persists — not that an impossible one does.
    del g.manifestations['manif:bad']

input.new_string:
def main() -> int:
    # A BARE INVOCATION runs the embedded self-test — the P6 acceptance entry point. It is offline, it
    # writes NOTHING, and every case is an attack on the core identity/version rule. Rebuilding the real
    # corpus graph (which reads the full corpus and writes the 28 MB GRAPH_OUT) is an explicit act,
    # requested with `--build`, never the default.
    if len(sys.argv) == 1 or '--self-test' in sys.argv:
        print('=== provenance.py SELF-TEST (every case is an attack on the core rule) ===\n')
        return self_test()
    if '--build' in sys.argv:
        corpus = json.loads(CORPUS.read_text())
        g = migrate(corpus)
        census(g, corpus)
        GRAPH_OUT.write_text(json.dumps(g.to_json(), indent=1))
        print(f'\n  graph written to {GRAPH_OUT}  (all text retained — nothing deleted)')
        return 0
    print('usage: provenance.py [--self-test | --build]   (no args = --self-test)')
    return 0

input.content:
"""Deterministic fixtures for the P6 real-chain 12-vector binding-gate acceptance battery.

Every builder here emits ONLY structural furniture (version stamps in the vocabulary of the world, a
front-matter DOI line, a byline) and per-domain identifiers. NO rule under test is allowed to key on a
DOI, title, author, venue, or subject literal, so the four DOMAINS below carry completely unrelated
identifiers across four unrelated fields (clinical / legal / economics / CS); the acceptance test
parameterises vectors 1-11 over all four and asserts the STRUCTURAL result is identical for each.

Nothing binary is committed: the salvage vectors (4, 10) reuse the identity_metadata raw-artifact
builders (PDF/HTML/JATS) generated at call time.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

# A long, complete scholarly body. 120 repetitions clears the scholarly stub floor so completeness is
# never the reason a vector rejects (except where a vector deliberately tests incompleteness).
FILLER = ('This paper studies the question at length across many pages of careful analysis, '
          'reporting the design, the data and the estimates in full. ') * 120

# ── STRUCTURAL VERSION FURNITURE — the vocabulary of the world, never a subject or an identifier ──
JOURNAL_MASTHEAD = 'Some Learned Review, Volume 12, Number 3, 2020, Pages 45-67.'
ACCEPTED_STAMP   = 'Accepted manuscript. This is the author-accepted version.'
ACCEPTED_CITE    = 'The version of record appeared in the journal; cite the published article.'
WORKING_STAMP    = 'NBER Working Paper No. 25682'
PREPRINT_STAMP   = 'arXiv:2401.01234'

# A shared, DIGIT-bearing span. Canonicalisation never touches digits, so an exactly-shared span is
# exactly equal in two documents and a changed one is not (the correspondence vector, 9).
SHARED_SPAN = ('The estimated elasticity was 0.42 with a robust standard error of 0.06 across every '
               'specification we ran, and the point estimate did not move when controls were added.')

# A header made only of PDF glyph codes: ZERO readable words, so the identity window is unreadable and
# the reducer cannot bind on it (vectors 3 and 4). It is NOT a stranger's paper — it is a font failure.
GLYPH_HEADER = '(cid:12)(cid:5)(cid:99)(cid:7)(cid:41)(cid:3)(cid:88)(cid:76)(cid:11)(cid:29) ' * 12


#: Four unrelated Work identities. Every identifier/subject term differs; the STRUCTURE is held constant
#: so the acceptance test can prove the outcome is determined by typed structure, not by any label.
DOMAINS = [
    dict(id='clinical', doi='10.1056/clin-A', foreign_doi='10.1056/clin-STRANGER',
         title='A Randomised Trial of the Widget Regimen in Adults', authors=('Adams', 'Brown'),
         byline='By Alice Adams and Bob Brown', venue='New England Journal of Medicine',
         foreign_title='An Unrelated Cohort Analysis of the Gadget Protocol',
         foreign_byline='By Carla Carter and Dan Dupont',
         generic_title='Adult Trial Report'),
    dict(id='legal', doi='10.2307/legal-A', foreign_doi='10.2307/legal-STRANGER',
         title='On the Interpretation of the Contract Formation Doctrine', authors=('Reyes', 'Silva'),
         byline='By Ana Reyes and Ivo Silva', venue='Harvard Law Review',
         foreign_title='Concerning the Distinct Rule of Promissory Estoppel',
         foreign_byline='By Omar Farouk and Lena Vogt',
         generic_title='Doctrine Case Note'),
    dict(id='economics', doi='10.1086/econ-A', foreign_doi='10.1086/econ-STRANGER',
         title='Automation and the Structure of New Labour Tasks', authors=('Okoro', 'Tan'),
         byline='By Ngozi Okoro and Wei Tan', venue='Journal of Political Economy',
         foreign_title='Migration and the Composition of Regional Wages',
         foreign_byline='By Priya Nair and Sven Holt',
         generic_title='Labour Market Note'),
    dict(id='cs', doi='10.5555/cs-A', foreign_doi='10.5555/cs-STRANGER',
         title='A Transformer Architecture for Program Synthesis Tasks', authors=('Park', 'Vasquez'),
         byline='By Jin Park and Luis Vasquez', venue='NeurIPS Proceedings',
         foreign_title='A Recurrent Model for Automated Theorem Proving',
         foreign_byline='By Mei Chen and Karl Ober',
         generic_title='Synthesis System Report'),
]

DOMAINS_BY_ID = {d['id']: d for d in DOMAINS}


def scholarly_body(d, *, furniture='', doi='__self__', title=None, byline=None, span=None,
                   masthead=JOURNAL_MASTHEAD):
    """A complete scholarly body. `doi='__self__'` prints the domain's OWN requested DOI in the front
    matter (identity proven); pass a foreign DOI to make the bytes a stranger's paper. `furniture` is any
    structural version stamp; when supplied it vetoes the published masthead exactly as a repository
    deposit that carries both does."""
    doi = d['doi'] if doi == '__self__' else doi
    title = d['title'] if title is None else title
    byline = d['byline'] if byline is None else byline
    doiline = f'doi: {doi}\n' if doi else ''
    results = span or 'We estimate the effect is 0.2 units (standard error 0.05) across 722 sites.'
    return (f'{title}\n{byline}\n{furniture}\n{masthead} {doiline}'
            f'1. Introduction\n{FILLER}\n4. Results\n{results}\n')


def row(d, *, fulltext, doi=None, title=None, authors=None):
    """A corpus row that REQUESTS domain `d`'s identity, wrapping the given bytes."""
    return {'doi': d['doi'] if doi is None else doi,
            'title': d['title'] if title is None else title,
            'authors': list(d['authors'] if authors is None else authors),
            'venue': d['venue'], 'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article',
            'fulltext': fulltext, 'abstract': ''}
